name: Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda de Gredos — circular por el Parque regional Sierra de Gredos
region_intro: Jogging routes around Navarredonda De Gredos are set within the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, offering diverse landscapes shaped by glacial forces. The terrain features pine forests, high meadows, and granite formations, with the Tormes River providing tranquil riverside paths. Runners can explore routes with views of mountain peaks and access to natural pools. The area provides a variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Navarredonda De Gredos The most popular jogging route is Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda de Gredos — circular por el Parque regional Sierra de Gredos, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 53 minutes to complete. This moderate route connects the Parador de Gredos with the town, passing through a beautiful pine forest. Another top favourite among local runners is Sierra de Gredos viewpoint – Tormes River loop from Área Recreativa Tamborríos, a moderate 6.2 miles (10.0 km) path. This route offers views of the Sierra de Gredos and follows sections along the Tormes River. Local runners also love the Running loop from Navarredonda de Gredos, a 2.0 miles (3.2 km) trail leading through the local surroundings, often completed in about 24 minutes. Jogging around Navarredonda De Gredos is defined by pine forests, high meadows, and riverside paths. The extensive network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging trails with elevation gain. The routes in Navarredonda De Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Navarredonda De Gredos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Navarredonda de Gredos offers a wide selection of over 40 running routes. These trails cater to various fitness levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance runs through the stunning Sierra de Gredos Regional Park.

Q: Are there easy running trails suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, there are several easy running trails. For a gentle introduction, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31871406" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Navarredonda de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 2.0-mile (3.2 km) trail leading through the local surroundings. The region's pine forests also offer shaded and picturesque routes of low difficulty.

Q: Can I find longer distance running trails in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a longer challenge, the area provides routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Paredes – Puente de los Seis Ojos loop from Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 12.7 miles (20.5 km). These longer trails often feature more significant elevation changes, offering a rewarding experience for experienced runners.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while running?
A: The running routes in Navarredonda de Gredos are rich with natural beauty. You can expect to see impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4437146" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;views of the Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, including distant peaks like Pico Almanzor. Many trails follow the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5758520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormes River&lt;/a&gt;, passing by features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5754260" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozo de las Paredes&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6213569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of Hoyos del Espino&lt;/a&gt;. The landscape also features extensive pine forests and unique granite formations.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1165047150/senda-del-pinar-de-navarredonda-de-gredos-circular-por-el-parque-regional-sierra-de-gredos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda de Gredos — circular por el Parque regional Sierra de Gredos&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.7-mile (7.6 km) trail that takes you through a beautiful pine forest.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Navarredonda de Gredos is excellent for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are ideal to avoid the midday heat, and the pine forests provide welcome shade. While winter can bring snow, offering opportunities for snow sports, some lower-elevation trails may still be accessible for running.

Q: Are the running trails in Navarredonda de Gredos suitable for families?
A: Yes, the region offers trails suitable for families, especially those with lower difficulty. The extensive network includes paths through pine forests and along the Tormes River that are relatively flat and easy to navigate, providing a pleasant outdoor experience for all ages.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running routes in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in the Sierra de Gredos Regional Park, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock. Always check local regulations or signage for specific restrictions on certain paths.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running routes?
A: Navarredonda de Gredos, being a strategic point for exploring the Sierra de Gredos, typically offers parking options near trailheads and within the town. The Parador Nacional de Gredos, a landmark in the area, also serves as a starting point for trails and usually has parking facilities.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Navarredonda de Gredos?
A: The running routes in Navarredonda de Gredos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.75 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the pine forests, and the stunning views of the Sierra de Gredos, making it a truly rewarding destination for outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the highest peaks?
A: Many routes provide spectacular views of the Sierra de Gredos, including distant glimpses of its highest peaks like Pico Almanzor (2,591 m). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31873789" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gredos viewpoint – Tormes River loop from Área Recreativa Tamborríos&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas of the mountain range.
tips: Small town at the foot of the Sierra de Gredos and very close to Río Tormes. The Church of Navarredonda de Gredos is one of the most important elements of the nucleus.
There are beautiful trails here and plenty of opportunities to enjoy the recreation area

Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda (PR-AV 19) is a short (7 km) but beautiful route that connects the Parador de Gredos parking lot with the town of Navarredonda. Pass through a beautiful mature pine forest of Scots pine (Pinus sylvestris).

It is of low difficulty, even cyclable throughout its entire length, without a notable gradient. Recommended throughout the year and for the whole family.

waypoints.name: Senda del Pinar de Navarredonda de Gredos
